description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a slightly rounded screen. The front features a large, green-tinted display area bordered by a thick, black bezel, with a wooden panel on the right side housing several rotary dials and push buttons for manual control. Two long, thin metal antennas extend upward from the top, indicating its analog signal reception capability. The casing is primarily a muted olive green color, with a prominent black bulge at the back for housing the cathode ray tube and internal electronics. Small, angled feet are visible at the base, elevating the unit slightly above the ground. The overall design is characteristic of mid-20th-century consumer electronics, with robust construction and utilitarian styling.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a green-tinted screen, black bezel, wooden control panel, and dual metal antennas. The casing is olive green with a large black rear housing and small feet.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the large black bulge for the cathode ray tube and the two metal antennas extending from the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the olive green casing and the depth of the rear housing, with the antenna visible at the top.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the green-tinted screen, thick black bezel, wooden control panel with dials and buttons, and the antennas.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain olive green casing and the antenna base, with less detail than the control panel side.
attribute_bezel_design: thick black plastic bezel with a wooden panel on the right side, matte finish, utilitarian styling
attribute_control_button_layout: cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the front right wooden panel
